storyInfo: Getting a handle on how her Sarkaz parents have been faring back in Victoria in their new home, Lava surreptitiously observes the neighbors, only to meet a little girl who calls her 'devil'.

attributes.text: \nHibiscus and I will be on the way to see you by the time you get this letter. No point writing to try and turn us back, we won't get it anyway.
\nI know you don't want to admit it yourselves, but I know why you had to move again in such a rush.
\nI know you say the fighting's long passed and your village is nice and remote and the villagers are all fairly tolerant of Sarkaz, but does that really describe anywhere in Victoria anymore?

\nBesides, according to you, everything's been just hunky-dory ever since the war in Londinium broke out. That's why we have to come see for ourselves if you're doing as fine as you say you are. If anyone's giving you trouble.
\nPoint being, we can talk when we meet. Promise me you'll look after yourselves.

attributes.content: Lava, you found the chili powder yet? We're not gonna make it in time for dinner if we don't start marinating the meat soon...
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Stop rushing me. We only need to get it done before they're home. Why's so much of their stuff in boxes...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: A harp? I didn't know they learned music.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: That's their friend's. The really good one, Mr. Baroque. I think they lost all contact with him after the war broke out.
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: ...Eurgh, they've still got our workbooks from primary school! I think we should just toss these...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: You can't do that! We're almost never home. Mum likes to flip through them.
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Flip through these? It's not like we never visit... here's a pile of photo albums—oof! Heavy!
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Are these... all from when they were working in Londinium? I don't recognize any of these people... I think by the clothes, it's all their coworkers. Every photo in here has been kept like brand new.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: And under that are all the letters. Honestly, you'd think after moving so many times they'd have thrown these away... nothing but clutter.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: This pretty much represents most of Mum and Dad's life, after all... we're not going to get it organized just like that. Could you ask anyone to sort out their entire life that easily?
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Not to mention they have to head out for work during the day...
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Not in here—not in here... maybe the shelves? Urk...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: ...Hibiscus, look at this.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: These are recruitment notices... typist, gardener, childcarer... crossed out, all of them.
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: They used to work as engineers...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Is living in Victoria really worth putting up with this...?
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: If you make them leave, where else can they go?
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Lava, put those notices back. And don't bring it up in front of them. We should just... focus on doing what we can.
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: ...Can't find the chili powder. I'll ask the neighbors if I can borrow some.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Wait—oh, she's just going to storm off like that... I needed to warn her not to get into rows with the neighbors...
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Hello, I live in the building furthest on the east. Would it be any trouble if I borrowed...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Er... no, sorry, we don't have any. Best of luck.
attributes.name: Neighbor A

attributes.content: (Won't even hear me finishing a sentence...)
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Hello, I'm wondering if I could borrow some chili powder.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: They flatly shut the door in her face.
(Tolerant... you say?)
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Hello, I'm living in...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: I know.
attributes.name: Neighbor B

attributes.content: Um...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: A curious child peeks out from behind her mother's back, peeking at the young Sarkaz girl at their door.
Ilsa, back in your room.
attributes.name: Neighbor B

attributes.content: We only just moved in not long ago...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: I know. You've been knocking on every door before mine. Got something the matter?
attributes.name: Neighbor B

attributes.content: My sister's making dinner, and we can't find where the chili powder went. Could we borrow a bit?
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: ...So that's all, is it? Wait there a sec if you please.
attributes.name: Neighbor B

attributes.content: The door closes, only to then creak ajar. Out peeks an inquisitive little head that stares at Lava—or more precisely, at the horns on her head.
The girl gulps somewhat anxiously, dithers for a moment, then reaches out a hand to Lava.
Lava is well inured to being looked at this way by now, and doesn't twitch. Bit by bit the girl's miniature hand reaches close, and touches on the corner of Lava's hem.
Wow—it's real clothes. Not witchcrafty ones.
attributes.name: Curious Girl

attributes.content: Excuse me?
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Ilsa! Get back here now!
attributes.name: Neighbor B

attributes.content: The mother drops what she was holding, runs to the door, and shields her child behind her. The calm has fled her face. It was bound to.
We don't have what you're looking for. You can move along.
attributes.name: Neighbor B

attributes.content: With a furtive rustle, Ilsa climbs out the loft window and excitedly sends the sign to her friends. Soon, more and more people in the street are coming to a standstill as they spot a strange scene unfolding in the distance—
An unwelcome Sarkaz with a chilly expression walking through their village, and a gaggle of children tiptoeing not far behind her.
The lot of them duck behind bushes, dodge behind trees, wink and signal to each other, and occasionally talk into imaginary walkie-talkies in their hands.
Reporting in. The devil's at the haypile in front of Tom's place. She's about to reach his front door. Over!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Roger that! Defense artillery is in place!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: Mind, children, you oughtn't catapult things at her. You'll be putting yourselves in great danger...
attributes.name: Concerned Passerby

attributes.content: At an adult's intervention, the children squawk like fowlbeasts and scatter, only to gather again soon after.
Tch. Listen, you lot...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: The devil's spotted us! Evacuate!
attributes.name: Children

attributes.content: Devil...?
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: (What a pain. I need to just scare them off.)
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Everyone, look, there's all shiny footprints down the street she went. What's that about?
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: Hold on, I'm gonna check it out—whoa, whoa, giddy up, it's all fire! Wherever she walks, it's catching fire!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: Lemme see—it is! All these footprints are lit on fire! That's amazing!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Hey, she's getting out of sight! After her!
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: (What, still not enough to scare you?)
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Lava stares briefly at the catapults in their hands.
Hmph, fine. It'd be more hassle if I got any of you hurt. Try this on for size, then.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Ah-choo—
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: She just sneezed fire! Whoa, now she's got all fire wings!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: Wowww—! So...
attributes.name: Ilsa & Rose

attributes.content: So cool!
attributes.name: Ilsa & Rose

attributes.content: ...Okay, you kids have got taste.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: (Sobs uncontrollably)
attributes.name: Timid Sick Child

attributes.content: Um...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Briefly at a loss, Lava rummages in her bag for a sweet to try and pacify the child, but then Hibiscus floats to mind, telling her children need to be on an empty stomach when testing their blood.
Screw her stupid sis! Dragging me out to help, saying these kids who just lost their parents in the war won't be that scared if they saw other Sarkaz. Well, they've been crying the whole time!
The relentless sobs throughout the ward leave her at a loss. For each and every day the war's still going, these cries will not come to an end.
Um... would you—like to see some fireworks?
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: (Sobs)
attributes.name: Timid Sick Child

attributes.content: I'll take that as a yes, then. Watch carefully, you only get to see it the once.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Ahem.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: May my fires bring light to the lands!
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: A firework formed of flame leaps to the ceiling, and bursts in the air over the ward. Lava steals a sideways glance at the children's faces. The fiery display enraptures them, and for just the briefest moment they forget to cry.
Then, the flame sets off the fire systems, and everyone is doused in sprinkler water. For a while in the ward, just a fleeting while, laughter takes the place of cries.
Children are the same no matter where, it turns out. A flashy trick takes them in.
...Is it really the same? No matter where?
Lava, welcome back. How were the neighbors?
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: The neighbors? I was just out to borrow some chili powder...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Do you feel alright after making the rounds? You didn't get into fights with anyone, did you?
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: I said I was just borrowing chili powder.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Lava? What are you looking for? Did you make new friends with the kids outside the door? You always were good with children—
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Huh? Gone already? What's that... giant bag of things she took?
attributes.name: Hibiscus

attributes.content: Reporting in! The devil's left the house! She's now at the garden, over.
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Whoa! Fire! Listen, the devil's using her flames... she just burned a log in half! Reporting in, the devil is building something massive out of a bunch of branches! Very suspicious!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: What massive thing? Speak clearly!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: I can't tell! Reporting in, the devil's now airing out this giant white sheet... no, some kind of white witchcraft material, I bet, and she's draping it over the branches! Intelligence officer, analysis please!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: Intelligence officer here! Hmm, based on my view, this is definitely... some kind of dangerous altar.
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: That's right, the branches are holding up a screen. Look, she's rolling the log over now. It's an altar for putting offerings on.
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: (Gasp) The village is in danger!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: (Curls up)
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: C-Coward! We need to keep her under watch, and protect our village!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Wait! She's brought something new out! Some kind of mechanical device, it looks like a... a projector? No, that can't be right! It must be a witchcraft casting device!
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: No sooner has she said it than the apparatus turns on, and upon the white 'witchcraft material' appears a roaring, skyscraper-dwarfing Feranmut.
Eeeek—!
attributes.name: Children

attributes.content: Boom! Bang!
In the scene projected on the 'material', a mutant Feranmut tramples high-rises into dust in an instant. The people wail and flee in despair, awaiting a hero to take the scene.
Lava, Rhodes Island elite operator, master caster, and Catastrophe expert. In this hour, any hope of the city surviving rests in her hands.
Look! Someone's here to save the day!
attributes.name: Children

attributes.content: She's fighting that giant thing head on! Hold on, doesn't she look kind of like that devil...? No, she looks... exactly alike!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: With a great swagger, Lava sits down on the children's so-called 'offerings altar' and kicks back, visibly enjoying the movie—
It wasn't any accident she brought this not-yet-released film along for the trip. She was meant to put a smile or two on her parents' faces with it.
For as much as she griped about the overdramatic plot being shoehorned in, it looks like the whole thing came together pretty damn well after all.
The children sneak glances at her, comparing her against the hero in the movie.
They can't understand any Yanese, but the special effects draw them in, and before they know it, they're inching closer, closer, until they're almost next to Lava herself.
(Heh. Now I know you kids have taste, I can show you what real showtime looks like—)
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Oh no, the monster's going to flatten the building! Run, hero, run!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Heroes don't run! Look! She's about to shoot fire at it!
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: Hey... you two, where'd she go?
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: Aaahhh, the monster's catching up!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Hey! Hey! Sh—she's gone! That—whoever she was, that neighbor of ours...
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: What?! She was just sitting here! Where'd she go?
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: The spot where Lava was sat is, to everyone's surprise, now vacant. Suddenly, a ribbon of fire rolls forth, leaping out from behind them, running toward the bedsheet screen. The children shriek.
Listen, this... this might be a real altar, I think! We got lured into it!
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: Run... come on... Ah!
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: But the children stop running, as the spectacle before them stuns them into silence.

In front of their very eyes stands a woman in freshly garbed combat gear, the image of a hero in the billowing wind. With an elegant yet ruthless swish of the hand, flames catch hungrily upon the 'witchcraft material', a simple repurposed sheet.
The Feranmut on the screen howls in agony, and is burned to cinders. Thereupon the flames extinguish, not having touched a blade of the greenery about them.
Whoaaa—
attributes.name: Children

attributes.content: That was cool! Miss Hero, have you actually fought giant monsters like that?
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Just now when you did that, right? Could you teach me that too?
attributes.name: Theo

attributes.content: Your battle clothes are so nice-looking! Like, they're so handsome!
attributes.name: Rose

attributes.content: Miss Herooo, I asked first, so I get priority—argh! ...Mum?
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: The movie winds to a fiery close, and the children realize only then that their guardians have been watching all of them from close by with tense gazes. Now they approach, and each drags their respective child back home without even hearing an excuse.
Ilsa, I told you not to sneak off by yourself. You're grounded tonight. Think about what you've done.
attributes.name: Ilsa's Mother

attributes.content: Stop pulling, Mum! Listen, I got to see this really cool...
attributes.name: Ilsa

attributes.content: Not a word more.
attributes.name: Ilsa's Mother

attributes.content: You don't have to run away, you know? It's not like I've got an extra pair of teeth or wings or anything compared to...
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Whatever, leave me alone with my peace and quiet. No kids to entertain. They're just noisy little brats.
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: What's this...?
attributes.name: Lava

attributes.content: Where Ilsa sat, there quietly remains a bottle of chili powder. Lava stares blankly, then lifts her head and is about to shout a thank you after her, but they've all long vanished out of sight.
So, with nothing else to do, she picks it up and walks on home, a small and easy spring in her step.
